posts.raw_content: This is the best conservative news site out there by far, this is why I'm taking the time to write this. I was with you guys on reddit, became a member of PDW soon after our exit there. Felt this site had to survive. It's starting to look a lot like reddit around here lately. Sometimes there are more "removed" comments than visible comments on a post. Every day I see people quitting. People who have been here as long as me. Complaining about the censorship. They all can't be "sleeper accounts".  I feel this community should police itself through the use of the up/down vote option. I agree or disagree with what you said. I used this option a great deal since this war began and as of yesterday morning, my downvotes no longer work (probably downvoted a "favorite son"). Wrote the mods, got no reply. If someone leaves a comment about why they disagree, they are just insulted by the other side anyway. Where are the mods on this? That is why we need the up/down vote, but it seems that is being controlled as well. Looking an awful lot like reddit around here lately.

posts.content: This is the best conservative news site out there by far, this is why I'm taking the time to write this. I was with you guys on reddit, became a member of PDW soon after our exit there. Felt this site had to survive. It's starting to look a lot like reddit around here lately. Sometimes there are more "removed" comments than visible comments on a post. Every day I see people quitting. People who have been here as long as me. Complaining about the censorship. They all can't be "sleeper accounts". I feel this community should police itself through the use of the up/down vote option. I agree or disagree with what you said. I used this option a great deal since this war began and as of yesterday morning, my downvotes no longer work (probably downvoted a "favorite son"). Wrote the mods, got no reply. If someone leaves a comment about why they disagree, they are just insulted by the other side anyway. Where are the mods on this? That is why we need the up/down vote, but it seems that is being controlled as well. Looking an awful lot like reddit around here lately.

posts.author: South_Florida_Guy
posts.title: Japan Nose

link_metadata.description: The tengu, an emblematic creature of Japanese folklore, has fascinated for centuries with its striking appearance and complex role in Japanese mythology. Half-man, half-bird, this red-faced, long-nosed yokai embodies both threat and protection, oscillating between malevolent demon and tutelary deity. From sacred mountains to Buddhist temples, the tengu has left its mark on the Japanese imagination, evolving over time to become a key figure in popular culture. Let's delve into the mysterious world of this long-nosed demon, whose history reflects the transformations of Japanese society itself.
link_metadata.title: The tengu: the long-nosed demon

link_metadata.description: The couple, Ugur Sahin and Özlem Türeci, will shift to the new company by the end of the year, BioNTech said Tuesday.  “The departure of the company’s founders is devastating news for the company and its shareholders” at a critical juncture, said Markus Manns, a portfolio manager at Union Investment and a BioNTech investor.
